The noema and the noesis are two correlative concepts in phenomenology that are central to understanding the structure of intentionality. The noema refers to

--- SOURCE 4: Husserl's Phenomenology and Two Terms of Noema and Noesis ---
URL: https://www.ssoar.info/ssoar/bitstream/document/57245/1/ssoar-ilshs-2015-53-shahabi_et_al-Husserls_phenomenology_and_two_terms.pdf

SUMMARY:
Noesis gives meaning to intentional act and Noema is a meaning which is given to intentional act . In other words, any intentional act has an "I-pole" or Noesis and has an "object- pole" or Noema.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 5: Husserl's Phenomenology and Two Terms of Noema and Noesis ---
URL: https://www.researchgate.net/publication/281359188_Husserl's_Phenomenology_and_Two_Terms_of_Noema_and_Noesis

SUMMARY:
According to Husserl, noesis is the real content, namely, noesis is real character, the part of the act that gives the character to a thing.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Noesis refers to the mental activities involved in consciousness, such as perceiving, believing, or desiring, shaping how we engage with the world.
Noema represents the intentional object or content of the act of consciousness, embodying what is perceived or thought about during the experience.

enriched_points.point_title: Dynamic Interplay Between Perception and Meaning
enriched_points.expanded_content: The interaction between noesis and noema reveals how perception informs meaning, highlighting the essential relationship between experiential understanding and conscious interpretation.
